concept: The Depth Model — a cross-section of the Gulf of Alaska water column where the hero is the probability instrument itself: a glowing depth-occupancy band that breathes with the day/night cycle, built from 13 years of Chinook salmon tagging data, that a trawl net reads to dodge accidental king-salmon bycatch.
references: Kurzgesagt's layered, backlit underwater/cross-section science explainers (depth, parallax, glow)
Bloomberg Businessweek data-journalism cross-sections (editorial-schematic clarity over a real physical slice)

taste_dials.notes: A quieter, more meditative story than the last two (a grid ledger, a ten-minute drone race), so motion intensity sits at 5, a breathing tide not a race. Density stays moderate-high (6) because the depth axis, probability band, and diel dial are all load-bearing data, but negative space around the water keeps it from feeling cluttered. Color boldness at 5: a restrained indigo-navy ocean world with one bold bioluminescent gold accent, not full saturation.

palette: abyssal indigo-navy water column, a bioluminescent gold band, moonlit silver for the night pass, one coral-dawn beat at the diel turn
metaphor: the depth model breathes: a probability tide rising and falling through day and night, and the net must find the one door it leaves open
honest_caveat: The model gives a population-level PROBABILITY of where Chinook sit by season and time of day, learned from 13 years of historical tagging, not a real-time tracker of any single fish. It cannot promise a given tow is clear, and no captain is required to use it. Drawn on screen as WHERE (the depth axis) staying sharp and gridded, while WHEN (this tow, today) frays into fog toward a ghosted question mark.
voice: kokoro:bf_emma (calm British, composed) — published; edge-tts en-GB-SoniaNeural used only as the draft timing reference, per config/voices.yaml workflow.
music_mood: contemplative, oceanic, slow-building with a hopeful turn — not glacial-ambient (already used for the beluga dispatch), not tense/dark.

fingerprint.palette: abyssal indigo-navy water column, a bioluminescent gold band, moonlit silver for the night pass, one coral-dawn beat at the diel turn
fingerprint.metaphor: the depth model breathes: a probability tide rising and falling through day and night, and the net must find the one door it leaves open

divergence_note: Unlike the data-brutalist parchment ledger of the grid/data-center story (07-02) or the orbital field-HUD countdown of the wildfire-drone story (07-03), this Dispatch slices SIDEWAYS into the water column itself: a stacked-layer cross-section whose hero is the depth model's own glowing probability readout, not a built structure or a swarm, moving as a scrolling time-of-day strip rather than an assemble-build or a radial countdown, in an indigo-and-gold ocean palette that is neither parchment nor spruce-black-and-ember.

shots.centered: the trawl vessel's silhouette on the surface line, dead center, as the SEASON CLOSED stamp lands on it
shots.means: opens on the stake: a fishery can close over one bad weekend

shots.centered: the 700,000+ tag-point cloud coalescing, dead center in negative space, into one smooth curve
shots.means: 13 years of scattered tagging data assembling into the model itself

shots.centered: the assembled probability curve, now the glowing depth-occupancy band breathing with the day/night dial
shots.means: the model resolves back into the water column it describes, and the honest caveat plays: WHERE sharp, WHEN foggy

shots.centered: the trawl net's headrope depth line steering around the carried glowing risk band
shots.means: the model's output becomes a captain's actual choice of fishing depth, voluntary, not guaranteed

shots.centered: the wide Gulf of Alaska at dawn, boats visibly spaced clear of the glowing band, then the branded outro
shots.means: pulls back from one captain's choice to the whole fleet, run after run, then signs off

beats.vo: A fishery can close over one bad weekend.
beats.shows: cross-section opens at dusk; a trawl silhouette glides the surface line; ALASKA.AI eyebrow settles

beats.vo: In twenty twenty four, two boats caught enough king salmon by accident
beats.shows: a '2024' date chip snaps in; two boat icons appear; a red bycatch tally ticks upward
beats.sfx: tick tick (tally counting), a rising riser

beats.vo: to shut down Alaska's pollock season.
beats.shows: the boats freeze; a red SEASON CLOSED stamp slams onto the surface line

beats.vo: Every king salmon they take is one that never reaches a river.
beats.shows: camera pushes down into the water; a single glowing Chinook silhouette swims toward a river-mouth icon and fades
beats.means: reframes policy into one fish's fate; carries into shot 2

beats.vo: So a University of Alaska Fairbanks team tried something new.
beats.shows: cut to shot 2: macro void; a UAF lab card assembles from points of light
beats.means: introduces the researchers, scene changes to the data itself

beats.vo: Thirteen years of tagged salmon in the Gulf of Alaska,
beats.shows: '13 YEARS · GULF OF ALASKA' label locks in; hundreds of tag-points bloom into the void
beats.means: establishes the dataset's scale in time and place

beats.vo: over seven hundred thousand data points,
beats.shows: '700,000+ DATA POINTS' counter races upward as more points bloom

beats.vo: trained a model that predicts where kings sit in the water
beats.shows: the point-cloud assembles and collapses into one smooth glowing probability curve
beats.means: the BUILD payoff: noise becomes a model

beats.vo: by season and time of day.
beats.shows: graphic-match cut to shot 3: the curve resolves into the water-column's glowing depth-occupancy band; a day/night dial ticks beside it
beats.means: the model returns to the sea it describes

beats.vo: It cannot track one fish, or promise one tow comes up clear.
beats.shows: the band stays sharp and gridded along the DEPTH axis (WHERE); toward a 'this tow, today' readout it dissolves into fog around a ghosted '?'
beats.sfx: a thin riser fraying into soft noise, a faint tick on the '?'
beats.means: draws the one honest caveat as a picture, not a text card

beats.vo: What it shows is the odds, so a captain can choose a depth
beats.shows: mask-wipe carries the glowing band into shot 4: a trawl net's headrope depth line steers around it; 'PROPOSED, NOT GUARANTEED' ghosted label
beats.sfx: whoosh (cut to boat), mechanical creak (net paying out)
beats.means: the model becomes an actual, voluntary choice at sea

beats.shows: a 'SAFER DEPTH · ILLUSTRATIVE' readout chip lands beside the wheelhouse
a plain 'VOLUNTARY' stamp lands, softer than the earlier SEASON CLOSED stamp

beats.vo: But if enough boats do, the same fish and the same nets could add up to a better bet,
beats.shows: pull-out to shot 5: wide dawn view of the Gulf of Alaska, several boats visibly spaced clear of the glowing band
beats.means: pulls back from one captain to the whole fleet

beats.shows: outro reveals: ALASKA.AI wordmark, tagline, source credit, staged beats, gentle cinematic fade with the aerial drift still moving to the last frame
beats.sfx: pop pop (wordmark, tagline reveal), a gentle resolve chime
beats.means: the branded sign-off, ending on motion not a static hold
